title: In Volyn, Plans Are Underway to Establish the Countryâ€™s First Hydrogen Plant Focused on Exporting Products to the EU.
content:
Austrian energy company Wien Energie, in collaboration with Ukrainian firm UDP Renewables, plans to establish Ukraineâ€™s first industrial green energy cluster in the Volyn region, with a focus on hydrogen production. The project, known as GREENWEST PtX, aims to produce green hydrogen from renewable energy sources such as solar and wind, aligning with the EUâ€™s long-term climate neutrality goals for 2050.

UDP Renewables sees growing European demand for hydrogen as a key opportunity, while Wien Energieâ€”owned by the City of Viennaâ€”is exploring new ways to expand its renewable energy portfolio, including solar, wind, geothermal, and hydrogen. The cluster will not only support local renewable energy production but also facilitate hydrogen export to the EU, with Wien Energie acting as both a hydrogen buyer and a developer of transport solutions to move the hydrogen from Ukraine to Austria.

This initiative reflects a broader strategic partnership aimed at accelerating the energy transition, replacing natural gas with climate-neutral hydrogen, and strengthening cross-border green energy cooperation between Ukraine and the European Union.
author: Analysts Account
